key_insight: A Neural-Spectral representation turns wave-equation control reconstruction into learning time-dependent modal coefficients, preserving the PDE’s oscillatory structure while enabling approximation of both state and control.
engineering_relevance: This offers a structure-preserving route to control design for conservative wave systems, with potential value in high-dimensional simulations where standard time-stepping can distort frequencies or energy.
method: Expand state and control in a Dirichlet spectral basis.
Parameterize modal coefficients with shallow neural networks in time.
Analyze errors from spectral truncation, network approximation, quadrature, and optimization separately.
evidence_signal: The paper provides approximation theory and conditional computable error estimates, and reports numerical experiments in 1D, 2D, and 3D on nonlinear, linear-reference, and high-dimensional control benchmarks.
watch_item: Performance depends on an exact control existing in the stated energy framework, and practical accuracy can be limited by spectral truncation and optimization error.
paper_title: A Structure-Preserving Neural-Spectral Method for Reconstructing Controls of Wave Equations
